title: senna pendula var. indecora
intro: Senna pendula var. indecora, also known as the lesser stinking cassia, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The medicinal properties of senna pendula var. indecora include its use as a laxative and purgative, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat digestive issues, promote regular bowel movements, and support overall gastrointestinal health. Senna pendula var. indecora can be prepared as a herbal remedy through tea infusions, capsules, or tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible natural remedy for digestive issues.
